instructions.instruction: Use Fully Qualified Collection Names (FQCN) for all module references (e.g., `ansible.builtin.copy` not `copy`)
instructions.original_snippets: Use Fully Qualified Collection Names (FQCN) ... ansible.builtin.copy ... Avoid - Short names (deprecated)
instructions.relevant_when: Every task in any generated playbook, role, or task file

instructions.instruction: Use `true`/`false` for booleans, not `yes`/`no`
instructions.original_snippets: Booleans | `true`/`false` | `yes`/`no`
instructions.relevant_when: Any task with boolean parameters (become, enabled, update_cache, etc.)

instructions.instruction: Use `ansible.builtin.dnf` (not `ansible.builtin.yum`) for RHEL 8+ / CentOS 8+ package installation
instructions.original_snippets: RHEL packages | `ansible.builtin.dnf` | `ansible.builtin.yum` ... ansible.builtin.yum is deprecated in favor of dnf for modern RHEL systems
instructions.relevant_when: When managing packages on RHEL/CentOS systems

instructions.instruction: Apply `no_log: true` to tasks that handle secrets or sensitive data
instructions.original_snippets: Secrets | `no_log: true` | plain logging ... Use no_log for sensitive operations
instructions.relevant_when: Any task setting passwords, tokens, or API keys

instructions.instruction: Set file permissions as quoted octal strings: `'0644'` for configs, `'0600'` for secrets
instructions.original_snippets: File perms | `'0644'` configs, `'0600'` secrets | world-writable
instructions.relevant_when: Tasks using ansible.builtin.file, copy, or template that set file modes

instructions.instruction: Never use `shell` or `command` module when a dedicated module exists; use the purpose-built module instead
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER use the `shell` or `command` module when a dedicated module exists ... ansible.builtin.shell: pip install requests instead of using the pip module
instructions.relevant_when: Any task that might be tempted to use shell/command for package install, file ops, service management, etc.

instructions.instruction: Every task must have a `name:` field starting with a verb that describes what the task does
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER write tasks without `name:` fields ... Always prefix every task with a descriptive `name:` ... Start with a verb: 'Install nginx', 'Copy configuration file'
instructions.relevant_when: Every generated task

instructions.instruction: Never use `ignore_errors: true` as a general exception handler; use `failed_when` or `block/rescue/always` instead
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER use `ignore_errors: true` as a general exception handler ... Use `failed_when` with specific conditions ... or use `block/rescue/always`
instructions.relevant_when: Any task that might fail and needs error handling

instructions.instruction: Never store secrets in `group_vars/` plaintext files; use Ansible Vault or an external secrets manager
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER store secrets in `group_vars/` plaintext files ... Use Ansible Vault (`ansible-vault encrypt_string`) or an external secrets manager
instructions.relevant_when: When generating inventory, group_vars, or host_vars files that might include credentials

instructions.instruction: Add a playbook header comment block documenting title, description, requirements, variables, and usage command
instructions.original_snippets:
# Playbook: <title>
# Description: <what it does>
# Requirements: Ansible 2.10+, <OS>
# Variables:
#   - <var_name>: ...
# Usage: ansible-playbook -i inventory/<env> <playbook>.yml
instructions.relevant_when: When generating a complete playbook file

instructions.instruction: Prefix all role variables with the role name (e.g., `nginx_port`, `nginx_worker_processes`)
instructions.original_snippets: Prefix all role variables with the role name (e.g., `nginx_port`, `nginx_worker_processes`)
instructions.relevant_when: When generating Ansible roles with variables

instructions.instruction: Use `include_vars` for OS-specific variables in roles (e.g., vars/Debian.yml, vars/RedHat.yml)
instructions.original_snippets: Use `include_vars` for OS-specific variables
instructions.relevant_when: When generating roles that support multiple operating systems

instructions.instruction: Use `state: present/absent` and dedicated modules for idempotent operations; when `command`/`shell` is unavoidable use `creates`, `removes`, or `changed_when`
instructions.original_snippets: All tasks should be idempotent ... Use `state: present/absent` ... When using `command`/`shell`, use `creates`, `removes`, or `changed_when`
instructions.relevant_when: Every task, especially those that might be run multiple times

instructions.instruction: Restart services via handlers triggered by `notify:`, not via direct restart tasks
instructions.original_snippets: Not using handlers - Restart services via handlers, not direct tasks ... notify: Restart nginx
instructions.relevant_when: When a task modifies a configuration file or package that requires a service restart

instructions.instruction: Set `gather_facts: false` globally in `ansible.cfg` and enable per-play only when facts are actually needed
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER use `gather_facts: true` by default for large inventories ... Set `gather_facts: false` globally in `ansible.cfg` and enable it per-play only when facts are actually needed
instructions.relevant_when: When generating playbooks or ansible.cfg for large inventories or utility plays

instructions.instruction: Add tags to tasks using appropriate categories (`install`, `configure`, `update`, `backup`)
instructions.original_snippets:
tags:
  - packages
  - nginx
  - install
instructions.relevant_when: When generating task files or playbooks with multiple operational phases

instructions.instruction: Use `block/rescue/always` for structured error handling with recovery logic instead of `ignore_errors`
instructions.original_snippets: use `block/rescue/always` for structured error handling with recovery logic
instructions.relevant_when: When a sequence of tasks needs rollback capability or guaranteed cleanup

instructions.instruction: When using non-builtin collections, include installation instructions in comments (`ansible-galaxy collection install <name>:<version>`)
instructions.original_snippets:
# Requirements:
#   - ansible-galaxy collection install kubernetes.core:2.4.0
instructions.relevant_when: When generating playbooks that use community or cloud provider collections

instructions.instruction: Use `async` and `ansible.builtin.async_status` for long-running tasks instead of blocking
instructions.original_snippets:
async: 3600
  poll: 0
  register: migration
- name: Check migration status
  ansible.builtin.async_status:
    jid: '{{ migration.ansible_job_id }}'
instructions.relevant_when: When generating tasks for operations that may take minutes (database migrations, large downloads, builds)

instructions.instruction: Generate a complete role structure including tasks/main.yml, handlers/main.yml, defaults/main.yml, vars/main.yml, meta/main.yml, and README.md
instructions.original_snippets:
Copy and customize the full role structure from `assets/templates/role/`:
  - `tasks/main.yml`, `handlers/main.yml`, `templates/`, `files/`
  - `vars/main.yml`, `vars/Debian.yml`, `vars/RedHat.yml`
  - `defaults/main.yml`, `meta/main.yml`, `meta/argument_specs.yml` (Ansible 2.11+), `README.md`
instructions.relevant_when: When generating a full Ansible role
